title: Nvidia And Other 6 Chip Stocks Set For A 'Secular Upside,' Analyst Says
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya identifies seven semiconductor stocks positioned for sustained outperformance in the AI era, driven by surging demand for AI chips and data center infrastructure. With AI data center equipment market projected to reach $1.7 trillion by 2030 (up from $264 billion in 2025), the key constraint is supply scarcity rather than demand. All seven stocks are rated Buy, with Nvidia offering the most upside at 71% to BofA's $350 price target.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: BofA rates Buy with $350 price target implying 71% upside; identified as top sector pick with strongest growth potential among the seven stocks
Rated Buy by BofA with approximately 38% upside potential; positioned to benefit from semiconductor scarcity and AI infrastructure buildout

title: Microchip Technology Stock Climbs On Data Center Revenue Growth
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Microchip Technology (MCHP) shares surged 10.36% in after-hours trading after disclosing its Data Center Solutions Business Unit generated $302.7 million in 2025 revenue and is projected to grow 65% in 2026 to approximately $500 million. The company's broader data center and compute segment represents 18% of total revenue. MCHP also announced selective price increases across its product portfolio to protect margins amid input cost pressures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 65% projected growth in data center revenue for 2026, significant expansion of a high-margin business unit, and strategic price increases to protect margins. Stock price jumped 10.36% in after-hours trading, reflecting investor confidence in the company's data center and AI infrastructure exposure.

title: Essential Performance and Real-time Control Without the Baggage
author: Microchip Technology Inc.
description: Microchip Technology has introduced the dsPIC33CK Value Line family of Digital Signal Controllers targeting cost-sensitive real-time control applications. The devices offer up to 100 MHz processing, high-resolution PWM, and 12-bit ADC capabilities at competitive pricing starting at $0.51 per unit with consistent pricing regardless of order volume. The family supports motor control, touch sensing, and precision sensing applications while reducing system complexity and bill-of-materials costs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is launching a new product line that addresses market demand for cost-effective real-time control solutions. The consistent pricing strategy, broad peripheral integration, automotive-grade reliability, and comprehensive development ecosystem support market expansion and customer adoption. The product positioning targets growing applications in industrial, automotive, consumer, and medical sectors.

title: Microchip Launches 3.3 kV HV‑D3 mSiC® Power Modules to Enable Solid-State Transformers for AI Data Centers
author: Microchip Technology
description: Microchip Technology announced the availability of its new 3.3 kV HV-D3 mSiC Power Modules designed to accelerate adoption of solid-state transformers (SSTs) in AI hyperscale data centers. The modules integrate silicon carbide MOSFETs and Schottky diodes in a 62mm package, enabling efficient power delivery from medium-voltage grids directly to server racks. The technology addresses the growing power demands of AI data centers while reducing conversion stages and improving system efficiency.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Microchip announced a significant new product launch addressing a critical market need in AI data centers. The 3.3 kV HV-D3 mSiC modules represent technological advancement with competitive advantages (reducing series-connected devices by ~50% versus alternatives), broad application potential across multiple high-growth markets (AI infrastructure, EV charging, rail/heavy transportation), and immediate production availability. The company leverages 20+ years of SiC expertise and provides comprehensive support infrastructure.

title: Is Everspin Technologies the Next AI Edge Breakout?
author: Thomas Hughes
description: Everspin Technologies, a producer of magneto-resistive random access memory (MRAM), is positioned as a potential AI edge computing winner with strong Q1 results showing 13.5% YoY revenue growth and expanded margins. The company secured a $40 million defense contract and raised guidance for Q2. However, analysts are split on the stock, and early May price action suggests a near-term correction to the $28 level, with key risks including dependency on government contracts and competition in the non-volatile memory market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings beat (revenue 200 bps above expectations), 13.5% YoY growth, margin expansion, improved profitability, $40 million defense contract award, and raised Q2 guidance support positive outlook. However, sentiment is tempered by analyst disagreement and near-term correction signals.

Mentioned as a manufacturing partner that enhances Everspin's capacity and strengthens domestic manufacturing footprint. Also identified as a potential acquisition candidate, but no direct impact on Microchip's business is detailed.
